behaviors.description: Data from all three sources is included in the content passed to summary generation
behaviors.justification: Unit tests mocking each data source independently would pass even if the aggregation logic drops one source. Only an integration test wiring the sources together catches bugs in data collection and aggregation logic.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the three data sources (metrics endpoint, issue tracker API, deployment log) to return known test data. Call the data collection orchestrator that combines them. Verify the aggregated data structure passed to the summary generator includes fields from all three sources.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, report is produced with a data unavailable note instead of aborting
behaviors.justification: Unit tests mocking only success cases never trigger error paths. Real failures occur when a source times out or returns an error, and code fails to catch it. Testing resilience requires integrating an actual failure into the flow.
behaviors.test_description: Mock one data source to return a connection timeout or 500 error. Call the full collection and summary pipeline. Verify the process completes successfully and the report contains a data unavailable note for that source, not an abort or exception.

behaviors.description: Narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data
behaviors.justification: If the summary uses an LLM, mocking it would hide hallucinations (reporting wrong numbers, contradicting facts) that the real model might produce. Accuracy depends on what the actual LLM outputs, not just correct prompt formatting.
behaviors.test_description: Create test data with specific known values (e.g., 5 bugs fixed, 3 deployments, 99.9% uptime). Call the real summary generation function (actual Claude/LLM API, not a mock). Verify the narrative includes these values accurately and contains no false or contradictory information.

behaviors.description: A complete report from data collection through email delivery reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly
behaviors.justification: Mocking email delivery verifies the code calls the API but misses real infrastructure failures (malformed headers, auth issues, SMTP rejection, delivery loss). Real failure means the email never arrives despite code returning success. Requires actual mailbox to verify receipt.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real test SMTP server (MailHog, testcontainers) or sandbox email service (Mailtrap). Call the full pipeline: collect from real data sources (or mocks of endpoints), generate summary, deliver via real email. Verify an email actually arrives in the recipient inbox with correct headers, subject, and body.

behaviors.description: When email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run
behaviors.justification: Unit tests mocking email success never test the error path and fallback mechanism. Real failure is when SMTP is actually unreachable, code fails to catch the exception, and never saves the file. Testing fallback requires triggering the actual error case.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the email service to fail with a connection error. Call the delivery pipeline and verify the report file is saved to the expected local location with correct content. Then mock email to succeed and invoke the retry logic (simulating next scheduled run). Verify the saved file is processed, sent, and cleaned up afterward.
